meta.purpose: Machine-readable processor-specific analysis checks for statement parsing, rule evaluation, and recommendation generation

processors.name: Heartland Payment Systems
checks: Verify analyzed month is representative using monthly sales graph before quoting savings
Capture interchange detail by card brand: txn count, volume, rate, per-item fee, total paid
Extract processor markup from HPS processing fee lines by brand and normalize to basis points
Flag unjustified AmEx markup premium versus other brands
Flag over-market markup (source guidance: above ~70 bps)
Detect monthly-vs-daily discount handling add-on and quantify bps impact (example: +5 bps)
Detect non-EMV trigger threshold (example: >10% non-EMV share)
Detect non-EMV fixed monthly fee (example: $25)
Detect non-EMV additional percentage markup on non-EMV/card-not-present volume (example: +1%)
Detect recurring Customer Intelligence Suite fee, verify usage, and flag auto-trial conversion risk
Run month-over-month new-fee appearance detection

processors.name: TSYS
checks: Capture card-type sales summary, credits, and total sales
Parse daily batch totals
Split blended rows where top number is processor markup and bottom number is interchange
Normalize blended rows into distinct interchange and processor components
Confirm pricing remains true interchange-plus/cost-plus after unbundling
Compare normalized markup and per-item economics against benchmark outputs

processors.name: Fiserv / First Data (Interchange-Plus)
checks: Scan fine print for explicit repricing notices and effective dates
Capture acceptance-by-use language in terms notices
Parse daily batch summary and fee sections
Identify processor markup lines (for example service charge labels) and normalize notation (0.008 => 0.80% => 80 bps)
Flag unjustified debit-vs-credit markup differences
Detect downgrade indicators (for example non-qualified, EIRF descriptors)
Estimate downgrade penalty impact (source guidance: ~0.3% to 0.4% extra)
Detect commercial card interchange savings adjustment fee
Quantify processor-retained savings share (example: 75%)
Flag hidden/opaque repricing risk

processors.name: Fiserv / First Data (Bundled)
checks: Detect bundled model using qualified/mid-qualified/non-qualified bucket presentation
Capture bucket rates and bucket-level volume/fee distribution
Flag processor-controlled bucket assignment risk
Flag over-market bundle rates (examples discussed: 4.09% mid-qualified and ~5% non-qualified)
Classify bundled pricing as high overpayment risk versus interchange-plus in most cases
Recommend interchange-plus conversion analysis

processors.name: Clearent
checks: Extract assessment markup (example: 64 bps) and per-item charge
Detect Express Merchant Funding premium (example: +5 bps)
Detect monthly minimum required markup amount (example: $25)
Detect actual markup paid in period
Detect top-up difference charged when markup is below monthly minimum
Flag monthly minimum risk for lower-volume merchants (source note: common under roughly $55k/month)
Detect PCI non-compliance fee and trigger remediation workflow
Track PCI completion requirements (SAQ, vulnerability scans, recert cycle reminders)

processors.name: Worldpay
checks: Extract basis-point markup by brand (source example shown: 86 bps)
Extract per-item transaction fee
Extract separate authorization fee
Combine transaction and authorization fees into true all-in per-item cost
Flag high all-in per-item burden (source context discusses >$0.40 as problematic in many cases)
Prioritize per-item optimization for low average-ticket merchants (source note: under ~$50 ticket size)
Detect monthly minimum language and assess effective-rate distortion

processors.name: Elavon
checks: Parse sales/deposits summary and daily batch totals
Scan notices for billing changes including online-only disclosures
Extract basis-point markup and per-item transaction fee
Detect additional authorization fee section
Combine per-item transaction and authorization fees to true all-in per-item total (source warning: displayed 10 cents can effectively become ~20 cents)
Flag transparency risk when fee changes are not clearly disclosed on-statement

cross_processor_checks: Compute net effective rate as total fees divided by total sales
Split all fees into card-brand costs versus processor-owned costs
Benchmark card-brand share and processor share against expected ranges from source context
Detect and classify unnecessary fees: non-EMV overlays, risk fees, unused intelligence suites, daily/monthly discount handling add-ons, avoidable PCI non-compliance charges
Detect hidden markup embedded inside interchange presentation
Detect month-over-month fee drift and newly introduced recurring fees
Enforce surcharge cap from source guidance (max 3%)
Enforce uniform surcharge rule across eligible credit transactions
Require robust debit identification controls (prefer BIN automation over manual-only handling)
Trigger full review cadence at least every 6 to 12 months
